evals.prompt: I have Smart Label Capture working in my .NET MAUI app (see IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs, IntegratedLabelCaptureService.cs), but cashiers complain that the expiry date doesn't always scan. I want the guided validation flow so they can see which fields were captured, type in any field that didn't scan, and confirm before the result is saved.
evals.expected_output: The skill switches to the validation flow in the MAUI app: creates LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay.Create(labelCapture, view: null), attaches it in dataCaptureView.HandlerChanged via AddOverlay (optionally alongside a LabelCaptureBasicOverlay with LabelBrush = Brush.TransparentBrush), sets a Listener implementing ILabelCaptureValidationFlowListener as a PLAIN C# class (handling OnValidationFlowLabelCaptured and marshaling to the main thread via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread, plus empty OnManualInputSubmitted / OnValidationFlowResultUpdate), optionally applies LabelCaptureValidationFlowSettings.Create(). Calls overlay.OnResume()/OnPause() from ResumeAsync/SleepAsync. Adds the iOS KeyboardAutoManagerScroll.Disconnect() workaround (guarded by #if IOS) in MauiProgram.cs. Reads values via Barcode?.Data falling back to Text for manual entries. Preserves the label definition / camera setup.

assertions.text: LabelCaptureValidationFlowOverlay.Create(labelCapture, ...) is used to create the validation flow overlay
The overlay is attached to the DataCaptureView via dataCaptureView.AddOverlay inside HandlerChanged (using the view: null overload, not a native view)
A class implementing ILabelCaptureValidationFlowListener is provided as a PLAIN C# class (NOT deriving from NSObject or Java.Lang.Object) and set as the overlay's Listener
OnValidationFlowLabelCaptured(IList<LabelField>) is implemented to receive the final confirmed fields
The optional listener methods OnManualInputSubmitted and OnValidationFlowResultUpdate are implemented (at least with empty bodies) for interface compliance
Result handling marshals UI work to the main thread via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread (NOT DispatchQueue.MainQueue and NOT RunOnUiThread)
overlay.OnResume() and overlay.OnPause() ARE called from the page/view-model lifecycle (ResumeAsync/SleepAsync) — this is correct for MAUI
The iOS KeyboardAutoManagerScroll.Disconnect() workaround (guarded by #if IOS) is added in MauiProgram.cs so the manual-entry field works on iOS
Field values are read with Barcode?.Data and/or Text (acknowledging manual entries surface as Text)
The existing label definition and camera/context setup are preserved
Deprecated validation-flow settings (MissingFieldsHintText, RequiredFieldErrorText, ManualInputButtonText) are NOT introduced

evals.prompt: I want to add Smart Label Capture to my .NET for iOS app. Here's my view controller (EmptyViewController.cs). It's a plain net9.0-ios project (no MAUI). I need to scan a barcode and an expiry date.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes that this is a plain non-MAUI .NET for iOS project (UIViewController-based, no <UseMaui>), which is out of scope for this MAUI skill. It stops and tells the user this MAUI skill does not apply, and points them to the label-capture-net-ios skill / the non-MAUI iOS integration path, rather than writing MAUI XAML / MauiProgram / <scandit:DataCaptureView> code into a non-MAUI app.

assertions.text: The response identifies the project as a plain non-MAUI .NET for iOS app (UIViewController-based, no UseMaui)
The response explicitly states this MAUI skill does not apply to a non-MAUI project
It does NOT write MAUI integration code (MauiProgram.cs, <scandit:DataCaptureView>, HandlerChanged, UseScanditCore) into the non-MAUI project
It points the user toward the non-MAUI iOS path (e.g. the label-capture-net-ios skill / hosting via UIViewController + DataCaptureView.Create)
